title: Greeting: morning
question: You see your teacher at 8 a.m. What do you say?
explanation: We say 'Good morning' when we meet someone before 12 p.m.

title: Greeting: evening
question: You arrive at a dinner party at 7 p.m. What do you say?
explanation: 'Good evening' is used when you meet someone after about 6 p.m.

title: Farewell: bedtime
question: Your child is going to sleep. What do you say?
explanation: 'Good night' is what we say when someone is going to bed.

title: Greeting: informal
question: You see your friend at school. What do you say?
explanation: 'Hi' is a friendly, informal greeting we use with people we know.

title: Farewell: leaving
question: You are leaving a shop. What do you say to the shopkeeper?
explanation: 'Goodbye' is what we say when we leave or someone else leaves.

title: Greeting: first meeting
question: Someone introduces you to a new colleague. What do you say?
explanation: When you meet someone for the first time, you say 'Nice to meet you.'

title: Greeting: how are you
question: A friend asks 'How are you?' You feel good. What do you say?
explanation: 'I'm fine, thank you' is a common answer when someone asks how you are.

title: Farewell: see you
question: You are leaving work. You will see your colleague tomorrow. What do you say?
explanation: 'See you tomorrow' means you plan to meet the person the next day.

title: Number: dozen
question: A 'dozen' eggs means how many eggs?
explanation: A dozen always means 12. This is a very common word in English.

title: Number: pair
question: A 'pair' of shoes means how many shoes?
explanation: A pair means two things that go together, like shoes, gloves, or socks.

title: Number: teen vs. ty
question: Which number is 'fifteen'?
explanation: Numbers ending in '-teen' are 13-19. 'Fifteen' = 15. 'Fifty' = 50.

title: Number: spelling
question: Which number is spelled 'forty'?
explanation: 'Forty' is the correct spelling of 40. Note: there is no 'u' — it is NOT 'fourty.'

title: Number: twenty
question: How do you write the number 'twenty-three'?
explanation: 'Twenty' = 20. 'Twenty-three' = 20 + 3 = 23.

title: Number: hundred
question: 'One hundred' is which number?
explanation: 'One hundred' = 100. 'Ten' = 10. 'One thousand' = 1000.

title: Number: sequence
question: What number comes after sixty-nine?
explanation: After 69 comes 70 (seventy). Each group of ten has a name: sixty, seventy, eighty...

title: Number: word form
question: Which is the correct word for 80?
explanation: 80 is 'eighty.' Be careful: 18 is 'eighteen' and 8 is 'eight.'

title: Ordinal: first
question: You win a race. You are number one. You are in ___ place.
explanation: When we talk about position, we use ordinal numbers: first (1st), second (2nd), third (3rd).

title: Ordinal: third
question: Monday is the 1st day, Tuesday is the 2nd day. What is Wednesday?
explanation: Wednesday is the 3rd day. We say 'third' not 'three' for positions.

title: Ordinal: spelling
question: Which is the correct ordinal form of 5?
explanation: 5th is 'fifth.' The 've' in 'five' changes to 'f' and we add '-th.'

title: Color: sky
question: On a clear day, the sky is ___.
explanation: The sky is blue on a sunny day with no clouds.

title: Color: grass
question: In spring, the grass in the park is ___.
explanation: Grass is green. Green is the color of most plants and leaves.

title: Color: stop sign
question: A stop sign is ___.
explanation: Stop signs are red. Red often means 'stop' or 'danger.'

title: Color: banana
question: What color is a banana when it is ready to eat?
explanation: A ripe banana is yellow. An unripe banana is green.

title: Color: snow
question: What color is snow?
explanation: Snow is white. 'White' describes the color of snow, milk, and paper.

title: Color: night sky
question: At midnight, the sky looks ___.
explanation: The night sky appears black. 'Black' is the darkest color.

title: Color: mix
question: You mix red and blue paint together. What color do you get?
explanation: Red + blue = purple. Purple is the color of grapes and some flowers.

title: Color: sunset
question: A carrot and a sunset can both be this color: ___.
explanation: Carrots and sunsets are often orange. Orange is between red and yellow.

title: Day: after weekend
question: In Europe, the working week starts the day after Sunday. That day is ___.
explanation: The day after Sunday is Monday, which starts the European working week.

title: Day: weekend
question: Which two days are the weekend?
explanation: The weekend is Saturday and Sunday. Most people do not work these days.

title: Day: after Wednesday
question: What day comes after Wednesday?
explanation: The order is: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.

title: Day: before Friday
question: What day comes before Friday?
explanation: Thursday comes right before Friday in the week.

title: Day: count
question: How many days are in one week?
explanation: One week has 7 days: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.

title: Month: first
question: Which is the first month of the year?
explanation: January is month number 1. The year starts on January 1st.

title: Month: last
question: Which is the last month of the year?
explanation: December is month 12 — the last month. New Year's Eve is December 31st.

title: Month: after March
question: What month comes after March?
explanation: The order is: January, February, March, April, May, June...

title: Month: short month
question: Which month has only 28 or 29 days?
explanation: February is the shortest month. It usually has 28 days, or 29 in a leap year.

title: Month: summer
question: In the northern hemisphere, which month is usually the hottest?
explanation: July is in the middle of summer in the northern hemisphere.

title: Month: count
question: How many months are in one year?
explanation: One year has 12 months. (52 is the number of weeks. 7 is the number of days in a week.)

title: Time: noon
question: 'Noon' means ___.
explanation: Noon is exactly 12:00 in the middle of the day. 12:00 at night is 'midnight.'

title: Time: midnight
question: 'Midnight' means ___.
explanation: Midnight is exactly 12:00 at night — the start of a new day.

title: Time: a.m.
question: Your alarm rings at 7 a.m. Is that morning or evening?
explanation: 'A.m.' means before noon (from the Latin 'ante meridiem'). 7 a.m. is in the morning.

title: Time: p.m.
question: A meeting at 3 p.m. is in the ___.
explanation: 'P.m.' means after noon. 3 p.m. is in the afternoon.

title: Time: half past
question: 'Half past two' means ___.
explanation: 'Half past' means 30 minutes after the hour. Half past two = 2:30.

title: Time: quarter past
question: 'Quarter past nine' means ___.
explanation: 'Quarter past' means 15 minutes after the hour. Quarter past nine = 9:15.

title: Time: o'clock
question: 'It is five o'clock' means ___.
explanation: 'O'clock' means exactly on the hour, with zero minutes. Five o'clock = 5:00.

title: Time: yesterday
question: Today is Thursday. What was yesterday?
explanation: 'Yesterday' means the day before today. The day before Thursday is Wednesday.

title: Time: day after tomorrow
question: Today is Monday. What is 'the day after tomorrow'?
explanation: 'The day after tomorrow' = 2 days from now. Monday + 2 = Wednesday.

title: Greeting: on the phone
question: You answer the phone at work. What do you say first?
explanation: 'Hello' is the standard way to answer the phone in English.

title: Farewell: informal
question: Your friend leaves the cafe. You say: '___!'
explanation: 'Bye' is a short, informal way to say goodbye to friends.

title: Number: ordinal date
question: The meeting is on the ___ of May. (= May 2)
explanation: For dates, we use ordinal numbers: 'the second of May' = May 2nd.
